skill.name: reasoningbank-intelligence
skill.description: Guides implementation of ReasoningBank adaptive learning for pattern recognition, strategy comparison, transfer learning, and AgentDB-backed persistence.
skill.summary: Build adaptive learning workflows that record experience, match patterns, and recommend better strategies.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution findings are false positives from Markdown code fences and JavaScript template literals in TypeScript examples. No prompt injection, malicious intent, network exfiltration, or real shell execution was found. The only confirmed concern is a documented local SQLite database path for persistence, which creates a low data-handling risk.

low_findings.title: Static command-execution findings dismissed
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts point to Markdown TypeScript code fences and JavaScript template literals. These examples do not invoke a shell, run external commands, or accept user-controlled command strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are visible as Markdown fences or JavaScript template strings in documentation examples. No shell execution API or command runner is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Local SQLite persistence should be reviewed
low_findings.description: The skill documents persisting ReasoningBank data to ./reasoning-bank.db. This is a legitimate local storage pattern, but stored task context and outcomes could include sensitive project data if users record it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The database path is explicit and the example enables persistence. The risk is limited because it is local storage in documentation, not hidden file access or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography finding dismissed
low_findings.description: The static high-risk cryptography alert maps to the frontmatter description. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, encryption call, or security-sensitive crypto guidance appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive text about adaptive learning and does not mention or implement cryptography. I found no evidence supporting the weak algorithm alert.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local persistence example
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented AgentDB configuration writes ReasoningBank data to a local SQLite database file. Users should avoid storing secrets or regulated data in recorded task context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The storage configuration and database file path are explicit. The concern is data retention, not malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need agents that improve from past outcomes instead of repeating the same strategy every time. This skill shows how to record experience, learn patterns, compare strategies, and persist reasoning data with ReasoningBank.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to initialize ReasoningBank with persistence and a learning rate.
Demonstrates recording task outcomes with metrics and contextual metadata.
Explains pattern learning, pattern matching, and confidence thresholds.
Covers strategy comparison, recommendation, meta-learning, and transfer learning examples.
Documents AgentDB storage configuration and metric retrieval for learned behavior.
limitations: Requires the separate agentic-flow and AgentDB libraries to run the examples.
Does not include executable source files beyond documentation snippets.
Needs enough recorded experiences before recommendations become reliable.
Local persistence may retain sensitive task context if users record it.

use_cases.title: Improve code review agents
use_cases.description: Record review outcomes, compare review strategies, and recommend better approaches by language and complexity.

use_cases.title: Optimize repeated operations workflows
use_cases.description: Capture task metrics and learn which operational strategies work best under changing conditions.

use_cases.title: Prototype self-learning assistants
use_cases.description: Use meta-learning and transfer learning examples to design agents that adapt across related task domains.

prompt_templates.title: Start a ReasoningBank setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the reasoningbank-intelligence skill to outline a basic ReasoningBank setup for my agent. Include persistence, learning rate, and the first experience record.

prompt_templates.title: Design pattern learning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design pattern learning for repeated task failures. Define triggers, actions, confidence thresholds, and how matches should be used.

prompt_templates.title: Compare agent strategies
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the skill to compare three strategies for my workflow. Explain what outcomes to record and how ReasoningBank should recommend a strategy later.

prompt_templates.title: Plan adaptive agent architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use reasoningbank-intelligence to design an adaptive agent loop with recordExperience, recommendStrategy, metaLearn, transferKnowledge, AgentDB persistence, and quality review steps.

output_examples.input: Plan adaptive learning for code reviews.
output_examples.output: A setup plan with recorded review outcomes, context fields, strategy comparison criteria, confidence thresholds, and periodic review of learned patterns.
A ReasoningBank workflow that records incident type, response strategy, time to recovery, follow-up quality, and recommended next actions.
A storage plan that uses AgentDB, names the local database file, enables vector search, and warns against storing secrets in task context.

best_practices: Record both successful and failed outcomes with consistent metrics.
Keep task context useful but avoid secrets and regulated data.
Review learned patterns periodically before trusting automated recommendations.
anti_patterns: Do not treat early recommendations as reliable before enough examples exist.
Do not record sensitive credentials or private user data in experience context.
Do not enable automatic learning without confidence thresholds and review.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build agents that record experience, learn patterns, compare strategies, and improve future recommendations.

faq.question: Does the skill include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and TypeScript examples that depend on agentic-flow and AgentDB.

faq.question: Is ReasoningBank the same as long-term chat memory?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on task outcomes, strategy quality, learned patterns, and adaptive recommendations.

faq.question: Why does it mention AgentDB?
faq.answer: AgentDB is used in the examples as the persistence layer for learned patterns and experience records.

faq.question: What data should users avoid storing?
faq.answer: Users should avoid secrets, credentials, regulated data, and private user details in recorded task context.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when the required libraries are available.
